title: In-Kyun Kim
text:
Who am I?
I am a HCMC Fellow at the June E. Huh Center for Mathematical Challenges, Korea Institute for Advanced Study. My primary research interests are in algebraic geometry and complex geometry, particularly birational geometry. More precisely, my current research focuses on the study of Fano varieties and their applications.

title: In-Kyun Kim
text:
Research interests

Algebraic geometry
Birational geometry
Fano varieties
Log canonical thresholds
K-stability
Kähler–Einstein metrics
Mixed Hodge modules

title: In-Kyun Kim
text:
Selected recent work

Wall-crossing for K-moduli spaces of certain families of weighted projective hypersurfaces.
Cylinders in weighted Fano varieties.
